title: Moire-Engineered Ferroelectric Transistors for Nearly Trap-free, Low-Power and Non-Volatile 2D Electronics
type1_summary: No evidence of cleaned, figure-ready tabular data files was identified.
type2_summary: The only file is a large PDF deposit with no reported tabular structure, so it is classified conservatively as Type 2.
classification_notes: The paper-analysis text appears internally inconsistent with the paper title/abstract, but the classification was made using the provided evidence only. No explicit source-data or raw-data statement was available, so the PDF was conservatively treated as Type 2.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The provided paper analysis states that no data availability statement, downloadable files, repositories, or URLs were identified, so there is no paper evidence that this PDF is cleaned figure/source data.
file_classifications.file_evidence: The file is a large 12.6 MB PDF from Zenodo. No tabular structure, headers, or figure-specific data organization is provided in the inspection report. Large PDF deposits are more consistent with raw or supplementary document-style content than directly replot-ready datasets.
file_classifications.reasoning: Given the absence of paper evidence for cleaned/source data and the lack of any file-level indication of tabular, figure-ready structure, the conservative classification is Type 2. The large PDF size further supports that it is not a small replot-ready dataset file.
file_classifications.ambiguity: Moderate ambiguity because the file content was not directly inspected; however, in the absence of evidence for figure-ready tables, a conservative Type 2 assignment is appropriate.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Not available from inspection; PDF document with no reported tabular dataset structure.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports the fabrication and characterization of twisted PbS moiré superlattices with strong interlayer chemical bonding, revealing twist-angle-dependent structural reconstruction and emergent flat-band electronic states.
